fixture_set: DC metro, HDHomeRun ground truth, 35 channels × 200 ms @ 8 MS/s
notes: STRICT gate. Achieves F1=1.0 (8 of 8 strong must-detect channels caught, 0 false positives) on the DC fixture set's STRONG channels (RF 9, 15, 21, 27, 31, 34, 35, 36).
These thresholds were grid-searched to maximize the worst-case margin across must-detect channels (i.e., how much each detector score could drop before the decision flips).

Compared to the prior hand-tuned recipe (pilot_snr>=40, pilot_sharpness>=25, vsb_asymmetry>=3) which had min_margin=0.16 dB on RF35, this recipe gives min_margin=0.76 dB — ~5x more headroom for capture-to-capture variation.

The binding constraint is pilot_sharpness on the empty side (RF7 sharp=27.10 vs threshold 26.25 → 0.85 dB safety) and pilot_sharpness/vsb_asymmetry on the must-detect side. pilot_snr=30 is loose by design: pilot_sharpness already rejects the only empty channels with high pilot_snr (RF7, RF12, RF20, RF26).
All three features come from a single windowed FFT — net cost is ~3-4 ms/channel on a modern laptop. A 35-channel sweep takes <150 ms of CPU.

Strict path does NOT catch the marginal channels RF 22 (MPT, Annapolis ~30 mi, sharpness 9.8 dB) or RF 25 (WDVM, Hagerstown ~70 mi, sharpness 4.7 dB). Strict is for confident auto-lock; the WEAK / --thorough path below catches marginals.

- field_autocorr: unreliable; any narrowband tone gives coherence at any lag, and adjacent-channel leak degrades real ATSC scores. Real ATSC: -16..-28 dB. Empties: -3..-55 dB (range overlaps). Not a useful gate.
- pn511_corr (original): broken — used the wrong PN511 sequence (didn't match canonical gr-atscplus atsc_pn511) and a naive baseband synthesis. Real ATSC scored 9-11 dB, empties 12-15 dB (inverted!).

- pn511_corr (fixed): now uses the canonical 519-symbol field-sync prefix (4 segment-sync + 511 PN511) as a Hilbert-transformed reference, mixes pilot to DC, and FFT-correlates against a 30 ms window. Achieves F1=1.0 alone with threshold ~22.3 dB. Cost ~42 ms/channel — at the budget edge. Combined with pilot_snr it gives min_margin 1.51 dB (best in budget) but for the same F1=1.0 with much less CPU, the 3-feature pilot recipe wins.

Future experiments worth trying: (a) capture multiple 200 ms windows per channel and require N-of-M agreement to harden marginal-margin cases; (b) an actual symbol-rate (10.762 MS/s) capture path so PN511 correlation has full bandwidth and tighter discrimination; (c) iteratively refine the spectral_mask with a per-station learned envelope shape.

thorough_thresholds.purpose: WEAK / --thorough gate: catch HDHomeRun-class marginal carriers that sit below the strict gate, accepting some empties as candidates that the user can skip from the picker.

RF22_MPT_Annapolis.comment: Caught. The negative vsb_asymmetry is the unusual feature — the upper-sideband data energy at this site is BELOW the lower side, opposite of the canonical ATSC shape. Possible causes: pilot offset is not exactly -2.69 MHz on this transmitter, antenna tilt favoring lower frequencies, or adjacent-channel leak from RF 23. Either way, sharpness 9.75 still says 'real CW pilot is here' — that's what the weak gate is keying on.

RF25_WDVM_Hagerstown.comment: NOT caught. RF 25 is HONESTLY indistinguishable from random noise in the FFT-based feature space. Its pilot_sharpness of 4.71 dB is below 10 of the 25 empty channels (RF 2 at 1.32, RF 3 at 5.46, RF 4 at 4.22, RF 5 at 2.50, RF 6 at 1.94, RF 10 at 4.59, RF 17 at 3.09, RF 18 at 4.16, RF 19 at 4.12, RF 29 at 2.64, RF 33 at 2.51). To catch it we'd have to drop the sharpness gate to 4.7 dB, which admits 13 empties as candidates instead of 9. Closest empty by feature distance is RF 30 (snr 19.00, sharp 5.99, asym 5.45) — distance only 2.54 dB across all three features, well within capture-to-capture noise variation. Conclusion: RF 25 cannot be reliably detected without admitting too many empties. Recommend the user note WDVM as 'present per HDHomeRun, below this scanner's noise floor' and either skip it, retry with a longer dwell, or accept that the antenna isn't picking up Hagerstown reliably.

fp_cost_curve.comment: Empties admitted as the sharpness gate is lowered (with snr>=15, asym>=-14):

alternatives_considered.tighter_no_RF22: snr=18, sharp=18, asym=1 → 8/10 atsc1, 7/25 FPs, but loses RF 22. The user wants RF 22 caught, so this is rejected.
alternatives_considered.tightest_catching_RF22: snr=18, sharp=9.5, asym=-13.5 → same 9/10 + 9/25 outcome but margins are 0.18/0.25/0.26 dB — too tight for capture-to-capture variation.
alternatives_considered.chosen: snr=15, sharp=8, asym=-14 → 9/10 + 9/25 with margins 3.18/1.75/0.76 dB. Worst-case margin matches the strict path's 0.76 dB philosophy.

recommendations_for_chasing_RF25: Multi-second FFT averaging: capture 5-10 seconds and average the PSD. The pilot is a CW tone, so it integrates coherently while noise integrates as 1/sqrt(N). Should give +6 to +10 dB pilot_sharpness on a true CW carrier and ~0 dB on noise. Cost: 10-50× more capture time per channel.
Per-RF threshold maps: bake in expected-RF-list from a reference scan and only weak-flag those frequencies. Trades generality for site-specificity but catches every HDHR-listed channel.

Use PN511 correlation: at full symbol rate (10.762 MS/s), correlating against the canonical field-sync prefix gives an ATSC-specific fingerprint that doesn't depend on pilot-tone strength alone. Cost: ~40 ms/channel and requires a symbol-rate capture path. RF 25's pn511_corr score in the existing 8 MS/s fixture is 12.31 dB (right at the empty floor), so even this won't help with current capture rate — needs the higher rate.

Antenna upgrade: the honest answer for a 70-mile transmitter. A larger UHF Yagi pointed at Hagerstown would lift WDVM's pilot 5-15 dB above the current capture's noise floor, putting it firmly above the empty floor.
